ud8.4:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud8.4:1.3:
And on that occasion the Blessed One was instructing, urging, rousing,
& encouraging the monks with Dhamma-talk concerned with unbinding.
ud8.4:1.4: The monks–receptive, attentive, focusing their entire awareness, lending ear–listened to the Dhamma.
ud8.4:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.4:3.1:
One who is dependent has wavering.
One who is independent has no wavering.
ud8.4:3.2: There being no wavering, there is calm.
ud8.4:3.3: There being calm, there is no yearning.
ud8.4:3.4: There being no yearning, there is no coming or going.
ud8.4:3.5: There being no coming or going, there is no passing away or arising.
ud8.4:3.6: There being no passing away or arising, there is neither a here nor a there nor a between-the-two.
ud8.4:3.7: This, just this, is the end of stress.
